test_results.goal: I want to become a full-stack web developer

matched_occupation.label: web developer
matched_occupation.description: Web developers develop, implement and document web-accessible software based on the designs provided. They align the client's web presence with its business strategy, troubleshoot software problems and issues and look for ways to improve the application.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Programming Fundamentals
objectives: Understand basic programming concepts like variables, data types, operators, and control flow.
Learn to write simple programs using a chosen programming language (e.g., Python, JavaScript).
Understand the concept of algorithms and how to translate them into code.
Learn to read and understand existing code.

learning_path.title: Web Programming Basics: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
objectives: Understand the structure of a web page using HTML.
Learn to style web pages using CSS.
Learn to add interactivity to web pages using JavaScript.
Understand the client-side nature of web development.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
objectives: Understand the purpose and benefits of using an IDE.
Learn to navigate and use a specific IDE (e.g., VS Code, IntelliJ IDEA).
Learn to use IDE features like code completion, debugging, and version control integration.
Configure IDE settings for optimal development.

learning_path.title: Web-Based Collaborative Platforms for Software Development
objectives: Understand the benefits of using collaborative platforms for software development.
Learn to use platforms like GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket for code sharing and collaboration.
Understand the concepts of branching, merging, and pull requests.
Learn to communicate effectively with team members using these platforms.

learning_path.title: Tools for Software Configuration Management (SCM)
objectives: Understand the importance of SCM in software development.
Learn to use Git for version control.
Understand branching strategies and conflict resolution.
Learn to use tools for continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI).
prerequisites: Web-Based Collaborative Platforms for Software Development

learning_path.title: Introduction to Web Services (REST APIs)
objectives: Understand the concept of web services and APIs.
Learn about RESTful API design principles.
Learn to make API requests using tools like Postman or curl.
Understand different data formats like JSON and XML.
Learn to consume and process data from APIs.
prerequisites: Web Programming Basics: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

learning_path.title: Using Application-Specific Interfaces (APIs)
objectives: Understand how to interact with specific application APIs (e.g., Google Maps API, Twitter API).
Learn to read API documentation and understand API endpoints.
Learn to authenticate with APIs using API keys or OAuth.
Learn to handle API rate limits and error responses.

learning_path.title: Working with Markup Languages (XML, JSON, YAML)
objectives: Understand the purpose and structure of markup languages.
Learn to write and validate XML documents.
Learn to work with JSON data structures.
Learn to use YAML for configuration files.
Understand the differences and use cases for each language.

learning_path.title: Using E-Services (Web Forms, Payment Gateways)
objectives: Understand the concept of e-services and their importance.
Learn to create and process web forms.
Understand the basics of payment gateway integration.
Learn about security considerations when working with e-services.
Learn about different e-service APIs.
prerequisites: Web Programming Basics: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript

learning_path.title: Debugging Software
objectives: Understand common types of software bugs.
Learn to use debugging tools in an IDE.
Learn debugging techniques like breakpoints, stepping through code, and inspecting variables.
Learn to read and understand error messages and stack traces.
Develop strategies for identifying and fixing bugs.

test_results.goal: I want to become a data scientist with machine learning expertise

matched_occupation.label: data scientist
matched_occupation.description: Data scientists find and interpret rich data sources, manage large amounts of data, merge data sources, ensure consistency of data-sets, and create visualisations to aid in understanding data. They build mathematical models using data, present and communicate data insights and findings to specialists and scientists in their team and if required, to a non-expert audience, and recommend ways to apply the data.

learning_path.title: Data Visualisation Software, Information Extraction & More (6 Skills)
skills: interact professionally in research and professional environments
disseminate results to the scientific community

skill_details.label: data visualisation software
skill_details.description: A computer tool or application that creates a graphical and visual representation of data, allowing a better understanding and interpretation of complex data through visual elements such as maps, charts, infographics or graphs.

skill_details.label: information extraction
skill_details.description: The techniques and methods used for eliciting and extracting information from unstructured or semi-structured digital documents and sources.

skill_details.label: information categorisation
skill_details.description: The process of classifying the information into categories and showing relationships between the data for some clearly defined purposes.

skill_details.label: query languages
skill_details.description: The field of standardised computer languages for retrieval of information from a database and of documents containing the needed information.

skill_details.label: interact professionally in research and professional environments
skill_details.description: Show consideration to others as well as collegiality. Listen, give and receive feedback and respond perceptively to others, also involving staff supervision and leadership in a professional setting.

skill_details.label: disseminate results to the scientific community
skill_details.description: Publicly disclose scientific results by any appropriate means, including conferences, workshops, colloquia and scientific publications.

objectives: Master essential programming languages
Learn database querying

comprehensive_guides.data visualisation software:
# 📚 Comprehensive Guide: data visualisation software

## 🎯 Overview
A computer tool or application that creates a graphical and visual representation of data, allowing a better understanding and interpretation of complex data through visual elements such as maps, charts, infographics or graphs.

## 🔑 Key Learning Objectives
- Understand core concepts and terminology
- Apply data visualisation software in real-world scenarios  
- Develop practical, job-ready skills
- Build confidence through hands-on practice

## 📈 Learning Path

### 1. Foundation Phase (25% of time)
**Goal**: Build solid understanding of basics
- **Theory**: Core principles and concepts
- **Vocabulary**: Essential terminology and definitions
- **Context**: Why data visualisation software matters in your field
- **Prerequisites**: Review any foundational knowledge needed

### 2. Application Phase (50% of time)  
**Goal**: Develop practical skills
- **Guided Practice**: Follow step-by-step tutorials
- **Mini-Projects**: Small, manageable applications
- **Problem Solving**: Work through common challenges
- **Tools & Resources**: Learn essential software/platforms

### 3. Integration Phase (25% of time)
**Goal**: Apply skills professionally  
- **Real Projects**: Work on portfolio-worthy applications
- **Best Practices**: Learn industry standards
- **Advanced Techniques**: Explore specialized approaches
- **Career Application**: Connect skills to job requirements

## 💡 Practical Exercises

### Beginner Level
- Interactive tutorials and guided walkthroughs
- Simple exercises with immediate feedback
- Concept review and terminology practice

### Intermediate Level  
- Project-based learning with real datasets
- Problem-solving scenarios from industry
- Integration with other skills you're learning

### Advanced Level
- Complex, multi-step projects
- Optimization and performance challenges
- Leadership and teaching opportunities

## 📊 Progress Tracking
- [ ] Complete foundational concepts
- [ ] Finish guided practice exercises
- [ ] Build first independent project
- [ ] Apply skills in work/portfolio context
- [ ] Achieve proficiency benchmarks

## 🔗 Recommended Resources
- Official documentation and tutorials
- Interactive learning platforms
- Community forums and support groups
- Professional certification paths (if applicable)

## 🚀 Career Integration
- **Job Market Value**: How data visualisation software enhances your profile
- **Portfolio Projects**: Ways to demonstrate competency
- **Interview Prep**: Common questions and talking points
- **Continuous Learning**: Staying current with updates

---
*This guide is personalized for your learning journey. Adjust timing and depth based on your pace and career goals.*

# 📚 Comprehensive Guide: data visualization

## 🎯 Overview
The visual representation and interaction techniques, such as histograms, scatter plots, surface plots, tree maps and parallel coordinate plots, that can be used to present abstract numerical and non-numerical data, in order to reinforce the human understanding of this information.

- Apply data visualization in real-world scenarios

learning_path.title: Data Mining, Mathematical Modeling & More

skill_details.label: data mining
skill_details.description: The methods of artificial intelligence, machine learning, statistics and databases used to extract content from a dataset.

skill_details.label: mathematical modeling
skill_details.description: The process of establishing a mathematical representation problem, such as equations, of a real-word to provide insights, discover new features about the problematic scenario, better understand the original problem or to make predictions about it.

skill_details.label: data ethics
skill_details.description: The subfield of ethics that assess whether data practices are considerable ethical. It assesses processes such as collecting, analysing and disseminating structured and unstructured data that might negatively impact the society.

objectives: Complete remaining skill requirements

comprehensive_guides.data ethics:
# 📚 Comprehensive Guide: data ethics

## 🎯 Overview
The subfield of ethics that assess whether data practices are considerable ethical. It assesses processes such as collecting, analysing and disseminating structured and unstructured data that might negatively impact the society.

## 🔑 Key Learning Objectives
- Understand core concepts and terminology
- Apply data ethics in real-world scenarios  
- Develop practical, job-ready skills
- Build confidence through hands-on practice

## 📈 Learning Path

### 1. Foundation Phase (25% of time)
**Goal**: Build solid understanding of basics
- **Theory**: Core principles and concepts
- **Vocabulary**: Essential terminology and definitions
- **Context**: Why data ethics matters in your field
- **Prerequisites**: Review any foundational knowledge needed

### 2. Application Phase (50% of time)  
**Goal**: Develop practical skills
- **Guided Practice**: Follow step-by-step tutorials
- **Mini-Projects**: Small, manageable applications
- **Problem Solving**: Work through common challenges
- **Tools & Resources**: Learn essential software/platforms

### 3. Integration Phase (25% of time)
**Goal**: Apply skills professionally  
- **Real Projects**: Work on portfolio-worthy applications
- **Best Practices**: Learn industry standards
- **Advanced Techniques**: Explore specialized approaches
- **Career Application**: Connect skills to job requirements

## 💡 Practical Exercises

### Beginner Level
- Interactive tutorials and guided walkthroughs
- Simple exercises with immediate feedback
- Concept review and terminology practice

### Intermediate Level  
- Project-based learning with real datasets
- Problem-solving scenarios from industry
- Integration with other skills you're learning

### Advanced Level
- Complex, multi-step projects
- Optimization and performance challenges
- Leadership and teaching opportunities

## 📊 Progress Tracking
- [ ] Complete foundational concepts
- [ ] Finish guided practice exercises
- [ ] Build first independent project
- [ ] Apply skills in work/portfolio context
- [ ] Achieve proficiency benchmarks

## 🔗 Recommended Resources
- Official documentation and tutorials
- Interactive learning platforms
- Community forums and support groups
- Professional certification paths (if applicable)

## 🚀 Career Integration
- **Job Market Value**: How data ethics enhances your profile
- **Portfolio Projects**: Ways to demonstrate competency
- **Interview Prep**: Common questions and talking points
- **Continuous Learning**: Staying current with updates

---
*This guide is personalized for your learning journey. Adjust timing and depth based on your pace and career goals.*

test_results.goal: I want to become an AI engineer specializing in deep learning

matched_occupation.label: artificial intelligence engineer
matched_occupation.description: Artificial intelligence engineer apply methods of artificial intelligence in engineering, robotics and computer science to design programs which simulate intelligence including thinking models, cognitive and knowledge-based systems, problem solving, and decision making. They also integrate structured knowledge into computer systems (ontologies, knowledge bases) in order to solve complex problems normally requiring a high level of human expertise or artificial intelligence methods.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Computer Simulation
objectives: Understand the fundamental concepts of computer simulation.
Learn about different types of simulation (e.g., Monte Carlo, Discrete Event).
Identify real-world applications of computer simulation.
Gain hands-on experience with a basic simulation tool.

learning_path.title: Data Visualization Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the principles of effective data visualization.
Learn about different chart types and their appropriate uses.
Develop skills in creating clear and informative visualizations.
Explore data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Python libraries).
Learn how to tell a story with data.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Algorithms
objectives: Understand the concept of an algorithm and its importance.
Learn about common algorithm design techniques (e.g., divide and conquer, greedy algorithms).
Analyze the time and space complexity of algorithms.
Implement basic algorithms in a programming language.
Understand data structures like arrays, linked lists, and trees.

learning_path.title: Artificial Neural Networks: A Gentle Introduction
objectives: Understand the basic structure and function of artificial neural networks.
Learn about different types of neural networks (e.g., feedforward, recurrent).
Implement a simple neural network using a library like TensorFlow or PyTorch.
Understand the concepts of backpropagation and gradient descent.
Apply neural networks to simple classification or regression problems.

learning_path.title: Information Extraction from Text
objectives: Understand the challenges of extracting information from unstructured text.
Learn about techniques for named entity recognition (NER).
Explore methods for relationship extraction.
Use regular expressions and NLP libraries (e.g., NLTK, SpaCy) for information extraction.
Evaluate the performance of information extraction systems.

learning_path.title: Task Algorithmisation: Breaking Down Complex Problems
objectives: Learn how to decompose complex tasks into smaller, manageable steps.
Develop skills in creating flowcharts and pseudocode.
Understand different algorithm design paradigms (e.g., top-down, bottom-up).
Apply algorithmisation techniques to real-world problems.
Learn to optimize algorithms for efficiency.

learning_path.title: Information Categorization and Classification
objectives: Understand the principles of information categorization.
Learn about different classification techniques (e.g., rule-based, machine learning).
Explore methods for feature selection and dimensionality reduction.
Implement classification models using machine learning libraries.
Evaluate the performance of classification models.

learning_path.title: Working with Unstructured Data
objectives: Understand the characteristics of unstructured data.
Learn about different data formats (e.g., text, images, audio, video).
Explore techniques for preprocessing and cleaning unstructured data.
Apply NLP and computer vision techniques to extract information from unstructured data.
Learn how to store and manage unstructured data.

learning_path.title: Principles of Artificial Intelligence
objectives: Understand the core concepts and history of AI.
Learn about different AI paradigms (e.g., symbolic AI, machine learning, deep learning).
Explore the ethical and societal implications of AI.
Discuss the future of AI and its potential impact on various industries.
Understand the Turing test and its significance.

learning_path.title: Creatively Using Digital Technologies
objectives: Explore various digital technologies and their potential applications.
Develop skills in brainstorming and ideation using digital tools.
Learn how to use digital technologies to solve problems in creative ways.
Experiment with different digital media formats (e.g., video, audio, animation).
Understand the principles of design thinking and apply them to digital projects.

Regular expressions.title: regexr (10,254 stars)

Logical reasoning.title: KAG (8,226 stars)
Logical reasoning.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs.  It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.

test_results.goal: I want to become a DevOps engineer with cloud expertise

matched_occupation.label: cloud DevOps engineer
matched_occupation.description: Cloud DevOps engineers implement and manage continuous software delivery systems and methodologies. This includes managing and configuring code repositories, build services, automated testing, and deployment mechanisms. For cloud-based workloads, a Cloud DevOps Engineer define and deploy infrastructure as code, automating test and development environments. They can define and configure automated disaster recovery solutions that meet business objectives.

learning_path.title: JavaScript Fundamentals
objectives: Understand JavaScript syntax, data types, and operators.
Learn about control flow statements (if/else, loops).
Understand functions, scope, and closures.
Learn about the Document Object Model (DOM) and how to manipulate it.
Understand event handling in JavaScript.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Computer Engineering
objectives: Understand the basic components of a computer system (CPU, memory, I/O).
Learn about computer architecture and organization.
Understand digital logic and circuits.
Learn about operating systems and their role.
Understand basic networking concepts.

learning_path.title: ICT System Programming Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the role of system programming in ICT.
Learn about system calls and their purpose.
Understand process management and inter-process communication.
Learn about memory management and virtual memory.
Understand file systems and I/O operations.

test_results.goal: I want to become a mobile app developer for iOS and Android

matched_occupation.label: mobile application developer
matched_occupation.description: Mobile application developers implement applications software for mobile devices, based on the designs provided, using general or specific development tools for device operating systems.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Python Programming
objectives: Understand the basic syntax of Python.
Learn about data types and variables.
Write simple Python programs using control flow (if/else, loops).
Understand functions and modules.

learning_path.title: Working with Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
objectives: Understand the benefits of using an IDE.
Learn to install and configure a popular Python IDE (e.g., VS Code, PyCharm).
Use IDE features for code editing, debugging, and testing.
Understand version control integration within the IDE.

learning_path.title: Mobile Operating Systems: An Overview
objectives: Understand the architecture of mobile operating systems (Android, iOS).
Learn about the key components of a mobile OS (kernel, middleware, applications).
Understand the security considerations for mobile OS.
Explore the different development environments for mobile apps.

learning_path.title: Introduction to Software Configuration Management (SCM) with Git
objectives: Understand the principles of SCM.
Learn to use Git for version control.
Understand branching and merging strategies.
Collaborate on code using Git and platforms like GitHub/GitLab.
skills: Git commands (clone, add, commit, push, pull)

learning_path.title: Internet of Things (IoT) Fundamentals
objectives: Understand the basic concepts of IoT.
Learn about IoT architectures and protocols.
Explore common IoT devices and sensors.
Understand the security challenges in IoT.

learning_path.title: Data Migration Strategies and Planning
objectives: Understand the different data migration strategies (big bang, trickle).
Learn to assess data quality and identify migration challenges.
Develop a data migration plan.
Understand data validation and reconciliation techniques.

learning_path.title: Using Application-Specific Interfaces (APIs)
objectives: Understand the concept of APIs and their role in software development.
Learn to use RESTful APIs to interact with external services.
Understand API authentication and authorization.
Handle API responses and errors.

learning_path.title: Developing Automated Data Migration Methods with Python
objectives: Learn to use Python libraries for data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL).
Develop scripts to automate data migration tasks.
Implement error handling and logging in migration scripts.
Optimize migration performance.

learning_path.title: Software Debugging Techniques
objectives: Understand common software bugs and errors.
Learn to use debugging tools and techniques.
Develop strategies for identifying and fixing bugs.
Understand logging and error reporting.
skills: Debugging tools usage (e.g., pdb in Python)

learning_path.title: Software Design Patterns
objectives: Understand the concept of design patterns.
Learn about common creational, structural, and behavioral design patterns.
Apply design patterns to solve common software design problems.
Understand the benefits of using design patterns.
skills: Application of design patterns to solve problems
